items.title: Two down, one to go - who will join Burnley & Wolves?
items.summary: With Wolves and Burnley now relegated, BBC Sport looks at which side is most likely to join them in the Championship next season.

items.title: Trump envoy seeks to replace Iran with Italy at World Cup, says report
items.summary: US special envoy Zampolli hopes for Italy involvement Doubts remain over Iran’s participation An envoy to the US President Donald Trump has asked Fifa to replace Iran with Italy in the upcoming World Cup, the Financial Times reported on Wednesday. The plan is an effort to repair ties between Trump and Italy’s prime minister Giorgia Meloni after the two fell out amid the American president’s attacks against Pope Leo XIV over the Iran war, the FT reported, citing people familiar with the matter. Continue reading...

items.title: Advantage Man City? 'Queen bee' Guardiola leads them to summit
items.summary: It took just five minutes for chants of "we are top of the league" to ring out around Turf Moor, as the title pendulum continues to shift towards Manchester City.

items.title: Ipswich Town stap dichter bij promotie na zege op Charlton Athletic
items.summary: Telstar en Sparta Rotterdam trapten woensdag om 20.00 uur de Eredivisie-speelronde af. Daarnaast speelde Manchester City in de Premier League, stond Bayern München in de halve finale van de beker tegenover Bayer Leverkusen en kwam ook Barcelona in actie. Lees het allemaal rustig terug in de VI Live.

items.title: Farioli mist bekerfinale met Porto na doelpuntloos gelijkspel tegen Sporting
items.summary: Francesco Farioli gaat dit seizoen niet de dubbel pakken. De 37-jarige Italiaan speelde met FC Porto thuis gelijk (0-0) in de halve finale tegen Sporting Portugal en dat was niet genoeg na de eerdere nederlaag in Lissabon.

items.title: Barcelona zet moeizame maar grote stap richting landstitel
items.summary: Barcelona heeft de voorsprong op Real Madrid weer opgebouwd naar negen punten. De mannen van Hansi Flick hadden tegen Celta de Vigo genoeg aan een door Lamine Yamal benutte penalty. Het wonderkind van Spanje moest wel geblesseerd naar de kant : de natie houdt zijn hart vast met het WK in het achterhoofd.

items.title: Taylor ziet zijn doelman uitgroeien tot penaltyheld: Lazio naar bekerfinale
items.summary: Lazio heeft zich woensdagavond ten koste van Atalanta geplaatst voor de finale van de Coppa Italia. De halve finale werd er in Bergamo een van de lange adem: voor de beslissing kwam het op strafschoppen aan. Daarin ontpopte de 21-jarige Edoardo Motta zich tot de held van de Romeinen.

items.title: Correia wil maandag gewoon trainen: 'De koning is volgend jaar weer jarig'
items.summary: Telstar heeft woensdagavond uitstekende zaken gedaan inzake handhaving in de Eredivisie. Trainer Anthony Correia wil na afloop van de ruime zege op Sparta Rotterdam niet gaan zweven.

items.title: Longstaff’s late show moves Leeds closer to safety in dramatic draw at Bournemouth
items.summary: Andoni Iraola may be on his farewell parade but it has not dimmed his passion as he edged Bournemouth one more point closer to what could yet be an extraordinary achievement in his final five weeks. Billionaire Bill Foley has held ambitions to take this little club on the south coast to Europe since he took over in 2022 and Iraola has made what seemed a fanciful dream a distinct possibility as he prepares to hand over the reins to Marco Rose. Continue reading...

items.title: Haaland steers Manchester City to top of table and condemns Burnley to drop
items.summary: Manchester City lead the Premier League for the first time since the opening week so the title is now a riveting five-game shootout with Arsenal. The Gunners were top for 200 consecutive days until Erling Haaland’s first-half goal ended the run and proved the difference against Burnley, who have gone straight back to the Championship. Continue reading...

items.title: Can relegated Burnley stop yo-yo effect - and do they want to?
items.summary: Burnley's fifth consecutive relegation or promotion leads to the obvious question - how can they break the cycle?

items.title: Jaden Philogene’s spot of luck gives Ipswich comeback win at Charlton
items.summary: After a sticky patch, the Tractor Boys are back on the right track again. Kieran McKenna’s side have shown a propensity to make things difficult for themselves as they chase the second automatic promotion spot and so it proved again after they found themselves trailing after just 42 seconds to Greg Docherty’s strike. Charlton looked like they might do their south London rivals Millwall a favour until Darnell Furlong scored his first goal since joining last summer with a brilliant strike before Jaden Philogene sealed the points rather fortunately from the spot. With two more away games to come in the next five days that will define their season, this was the perfect response from an Ipswich team that has found things difficult on the road since being relegated last year. While defeat here means Nathan Jones’s side still need a point from their final two matches to guarantee they and not Oxford will play Championship football next year, a third promotion in four seasons is in touching distance for McKenna. Continue reading...

items.title: Liam Rosenior chewed up by BlueCo’s bizarre ChatGPT version of Chelsea
items.summary: While the novice coach was clearly not a good fit, the lesson here is that billionaire owners are not always right after all Run Liam, run. Don’t look back. Wrench off the hazmat suit. Scoot past the security gates where the guards are already writhing and frothing at the mouth. And exit the compound for good, ice-white trainers pounding the dirt track, designer hoodie flapping. For Liam Rosenior the urge now must be to put as much distance as possible between himself and what is, if not the strangest and most illiterate footballing project of all time, then surely the strangest and most illiterate yet. Welcome to BlueCo Chelsea, a place where blaming the manager for the on-field spectacle feels a bit like complaining that the scientists inside the Chernobyl nuclear plant still haven’t washed up the canteen coffee cups. Continue reading...

items.title: Respecting the ball, losing the players - inside Rosenior's reign
items.summary: Liam Rosenior lasted just 106 days at Chelsea. So what went wrong? And how did it go wrong so quickly?

items.title: Hapless Chelsea owners have built a monument to chaos and decline
items.summary: Chelsea owners BlueCo have built a monument to chaos and decline at Stamford Bridge, says BBC Sport chief football writer Phil McNulty.

items.title: 🎥 Eén op twaalf in de play-offs: Vanderbiest legt pijnpunt van KV Mechelen bloot
items.summary: KV Mechelen heeft een derde nederlaag op rij geleden in de Champions' Play-offs. Tegen Club Brugge hield de ploeg van Fred Vanderbiest een helft gelijke tred, maar na de rust gingen ze kopje onder.

items.title: "Hebben we twee dagen mogen horen": Jackers prikt nu Club weer mag dromen van de titel
items.summary: Club Brugge heeft overtuigend met 6-1 gewonnen van KV Mechelen en mag weer dromen van de titel. Voor doelman Nordin Jackers verandert er echter weinig, ook al is de kloof met Union nu gehalveerd.

items.title: Un point vaut toujours mieux que rien dans les mauvais jours : la folle série de l'Union depuis deux ans
items.summary: L'Union Saint-Gilloise n'a pas réussi à trouver la faille contre La Gantoise. C'est un moindre mal, l'équipe poursuit sa série d'invincibilité en Playoffs.

items.title: Mats Rits hoeft niét te zoeken naar een nieuwe club: 'Deze Belgische club verwelkomt middenvelder met open armen'
items.summary: Mats Rits vertrekt op het einde van het seizoen bij RSC Anderlecht. De middenvelder kan rekenen op de interesse van een andere Belgische club.

items.title: Club Brugge walst over KV Mechelen in de tweede helft en mag weer dromen van de titel
items.summary: Club Brugge mag weer dromen van de titel. Het walste zelf over KV Mechelen en dankzij puntenverlies van Union komt blauw-zwart weer tot op twee punten van de leider.

items.title: Vincent Kompany et le Bayern en finale de la Coupe et encore en course pour un quadruplé historique !
items.summary: Grâce aux buts de Harry Kane et Luis Diaz, le Bayern Munich s'est qualifié pour la finale de la Coupe d'Allemagne, quelques jours seulement après avoir décroché son 35e titre de champion.

items.title: Après la déconvenue du week-end, Bruges passe ses nerfs sur Malines et revient à deux points de l'Union
items.summary: Encore frustré par sa défaite à l'Union, le Club de Bruges a passé ses nerfs sur Malines, ce mercredi soir au Jan Breydelstadion. Un large succès qui permet aux Blauw & Zwart de revenir à deux petites longueurs de la tête du championnat.

items.title: La Gazzetta dello Sport weet het zeker: 'AC Milan heeft alle troeven in handen om Club Brugge-sterkhouder binnen te halen'
items.summary: AC Milan gaat vol op het gaspedaal staan in de strijd om Nicolo Tresoldi. De Italiaanse topclub beseft dat het nu of nooit is om de aanvaller naar San Siro te halen.

items.title: Le coup de la panne : l'Union perd la moitié de son écart sur le Club de Bruges
items.summary: L'Union Saint-Gilloise n'a pas confirmé sa victoire contre le Club de Bruges, concédant le 0-0 contre La Gantoise. La fatigue semble avoir pesé lourd.

items.title: "Je supporterai l'équipe depuis la maison" : un protégé de Vincent Kompany forfait pour la Coupe du monde
items.summary: Mauvaise nouvelle pour le Bayern et pour la Mannschaft : la saison de Serge Gnabry est terminée. Victime d'une lésion de l'adducteur, l'attaquant allemand sera écarté des terrains pendant trois mois.

items.title: Longstaff's late, late show puts Leeds on brink of safety with Bournemouth draw
items.summary: Sean Longstaff's leveller with the final kick of the game put Leeds on the brink of Premier League survival with a valuable point in a 2-2 draw at Bournemouth, which dented the Cherries' European dreams.

items.title: Bournemouth latest: 'I cannot accept Leeds goal - Iraola fumes at 'massive' dropped points
Merse: Arsenal back in title race control despite dropping to second
items.summary: In his latest column, Sky Sports' Paul Merson says the Premier League title race could come down to goal difference - especially after Manchester City only beat Burnley 1-0.

items.title: Man City struggle but go top of the Premier League and relegate Burnley
items.summary: It wasn't spectacular but Manchester City went top of the Premier League table with a 1-0 win at Turf Moor - a result which knocked title-rivals Arsenal off the summit and relegated Burnley back to the Championship.

items.title: Football latest: Kane helps Bayern Munich reach the DFB-Pokal final
